mappings.rationaleAI: Enzymes as biological catalysts with Michaelis-Menten kinetics, lock-and-key models, and allosteric regulation directly address catalysts affecting reaction rates (C.1.d) and factors influencing reaction course (D.1).
Oxygen's role in combustion and oxidation, and Lavoisier's dismantling of phlogiston theory address oxidation-reduction reactions (F) and their classification (A.3.a.vi).

Free radical chain reactions in oxidation processes and their role in biological systems (lipid peroxidation, immune defense) address chain reactions (A.3.a.iii) and oxidation-reduction in biological systems (F.5).

Photosynthesis as a photochemical reaction converting light energy to chemical energy addresses the photochemical process (G), and its electron transport chain involves oxidation-reduction in biological systems (F.5).

Water as a universal solvent and its role as a medium for biochemical reactions addresses solvent effects on reaction rates (C.1.a), and water's proton-donor/acceptor properties relate to acid-base equilibria (E).
The second law of thermodynamics, entropy, and free energy changes are directly relevant to energy changes in chemical reactions (B) and thermodynamic relations including chemical equilibrium (B.3).
The history of heat science including the laws of thermodynamics and the mechanical equivalent of heat addresses energy changes in chemical reactions (B) and classification by energy changes (B.1).
